name: No-Knead Cheddar Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the Cheddar cheese and set aside in a small bowl covered to prevent drying.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups unsifted flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Mix thoroughly.
3. In a saucepan, heat 3/4 cup milk, water, and butter until the liquids reach a temperature of 115 to 120 degrees F.
4. Gradually add the warm liquids to the dry ingredients in the mixing bowl, beating for 2 minutes at medium speed of an electric mixer. Scrape the sides of the bowl occasionally.
5. Add 1 cup unsifted flour and beat on high speed until the dough is soft but slightly sticky.
6. Cover the dough with wax paper and a clean towel, and let it rise in a warm place until doubled, about 45 to 60 minutes.
7. Generously grease several baking sheets.
8. Melt butter and set aside.
9. Punch down the dough using your fist and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide into two equal portions.
10. Set one portion aside. Roll the remaining dough into a rectangle measuring approximately 16 x 8 inches.
11. Brush with about half of the melted butter and sprinkle with about half of the grated Cheddar cheese.
12. Cut crosswise into 8 equal pieces, then cut each piece in half lengthwise to create strips.
13. Fold each strip into thirds, lapping each side portion over the center third.
14. Place the rolls on a baking sheet. Repeat with the other half of the dough.
15. Beat the egg yolk with a tablespoon of milk until slightly blended. Brush the tops of the rolls with this mixture.
16. Let rise for about 30 minutes, then bake at 425 degrees F (220 C) for approximately 8 minutes or until golden brown. Serve hot.
